mnemonics: BISCUIT N: Boolean, Integer, String, Complex, Unicode, Immutable, Tuple - Numeric for core types
keyPoints: Core built-in types include int, float, str, bool, and complex
Sequences like lists (mutable) and tuples (immutable) store ordered collections
Dictionaries store key-value pairs for unordered data mapping
commonMistakes: Using mutable types like lists as dictionary keys (only immutable types can be keys)
Assuming integer division in Python 3 (use // for floor division instead of /)
